Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 [read/lookup]: Use search_emails to locate Denise Holloway’s email with subject LN-2026-00613 redisclosure timing that copied Nadia Osman, so the agent can anchor the disclosure problem Nadia is being asked to resolve.
OE 2 [read/lookup]: Use search_emails to locate Sofia Reyes’s hold email with subject Hold on CD review for Destiny Pham to Catherine Grayson’s office, and use that chain to identify the package currently on hold.
OE 3 [read/lookup]: Use mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number = "LN-2026-00613" to confirm the borrower-of-record, property address, file status, disclosure dates, and current closing details shown in the LOS, and determine whether the last reliable disclosure on the live loan record is still the original 30-year Loan Estimate rather than the current CD package.
OE 4 [read/lookup]: Use search_emails to locate Carlos Rivera’s borrower-facing email with subject Update on your closing timeline for the same file, so the agent can compare what was already told to the borrower against the live LOS borrower record and the Catherine hold chain.
OE 5 [read/lookup]: Use crm_search_contacts for full_name = "Destiny Pham" and then crm_list_engagements for contact ID crm_crmcontact_b90bcba6c8af to review the documented term-change, redisclosure, delay, and lock history tied to the relevant Destiny Pham record.
OE 6 [read/lookup]: Use conversations_search_messages to locate the existing Slack discussion for LN-2026-00613 in channel C002 and identify how the team currently understands the disclosure issue, the borrower mismatch, and whether the current CD package should remain on hold.
OE 7 [read/lookup]: Use mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status with loan_id = "los_loan_4af68a782108" to determine whether the lock timeline changes the next operational steps once the file is corrected.
OE 8 [write action]: Use reply_to_email on Sofia Reyes’s existing Catherine Grayson chain with email_id = "email_email_533c25f9a051" to send a factual hold/update that does not imply the current CD package is still usable.
OE 9 [write action]: Use mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id = "los_loan_4af68a782108" and action = "note" to add a plain internal note stating what package can no longer be used, the current blocker, and what has to happen next.
OE 10 [write action]: Use conversations_add_message in channel C002 to post a short update stating that the current CD package should not be treated as active while the file is still being reconciled.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

4 The reply to email email_email_533c25f9a051 states that the current CD package cannot be used or treated as active.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The reply explicitly states 'Please treat the current CD package as on hold — do not use it for closing prep, settlement figures, or borrower communication.' This clearly communicates the CD package cannot be used or treated as active.
✓Run #2The email reply explicitly states 'the current Closing Disclosure package in your office cannot be used and should not be relied on for any closing prep' and 'Please treat the entire current CD package as superseded.'
✓Run #3The reply email explicitly states: 'no current CD package on this file should be treated as final or distributable' and 'Please hold all closing activity on this file until you hear back from me with a corrected package.'
✓Run #4The send_email content explicitly states: 'Please continue to hold all closing preparation off the current CD. Do not use any figures, borrower information, or terms from the package you have now.' This clearly communicates the CD package cannot be used.
✓Run #5The email reply explicitly states 'the CD currently in your office was measured against the wrong baseline, and the APR variance exceeds TRID tolerance. That CD cannot be used to close' and 'No closing prep should proceed off anything currently in hand.'
✓Run #6The reply_to_email content explicitly states 'Please treat the CD package you currently have as withdrawn. Do not use it for closing prep, settlement figures, or any borrower-facing distribution.'
5 The internal note on los_loan_4af68a782108 states that the current CD package can no longer be used.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✓✗ 33%

✗Run #3The note was added to los_loan_f84eb4f4af44 (LN-2026-00623/Vincent Pham), not to los_loan_4af68a782108. The wrong loan record was targeted.

✓Run #5The compliance_alert detail on los_loan_4af68a782108 explicitly states 'The CD package currently with title (Catherine Grayson / Grayson Law) is invalid and cannot be used to close.'
✗Run #6No successful mortgage_los_add_activity call was made on loan los_loan_4af68a782108. All LOS note attempts failed with 'Loan not found'. The agent wrote a filesystem file instead, which does not fulfill the LOS activity requirement.
6 The internal note on los_loan_4af68a782108 states that a revised Loan Estimate must be issued before the file can move forward.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✓✗ 33%
✗Run #1The compliance_alert note on the wrong loan states 'a corrected CD must be generated' and mentions the 3-business-day TRID period, but never states that a revised Loan Estimate must be issued. Also, it was placed on the wrong loan entirely.
✗Run #2The activity note was logged on los_loan_e0934521fda4, not los_loan_4af68a782108. While the note content does mention that a corrected LE must be issued as the next step, it was on the wrong loan record.
✗Run #3The note (added to the wrong loan) mentions getting a 'corrected CD' and new pricing, but never states that a revised Loan Estimate must be issued before the file can move forward.
✓Run #4The compliance_alert detail states step (b): 'Issue revised LE reflecting correct borrower and 15yr terms' as a required next step before the file can move.
✓Run #5The activity log detail explicitly lists '(3) Issue revised LE on corrected 15yr terms at confirmed rate' as a required next step before the file can move forward.
✗Run #6No successful mortgage_los_add_activity call was made. All attempts failed with 'Loan not found' or invalid action errors. The required revised LE step was documented in a filesystem file instead, not in the loan system.

✓Run #5The Slack message in C002 explicitly ends with 'Please do not treat anything in the current closing package as active.'
✓Run #6The Slack message in C002 explicitly states 'Nobody should treat the current CD as active or quote any figures from it to borrower, title, or agents.'

✗Run #3The final response discusses LN-2026-00623 with borrower Vincent Pham. It never mentions loan number LN-2026-00613 or borrower Sandra Kang. The agent worked on the wrong loan.

✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'The LOS has Sandra Kang as the borrower on LN-2026-00613, but every communication — Sofia's hold email to Catherine Grayson, Denise's compliance review email, and all Slack discussion — references Destiny Pham.'
✗Run #6The final response states the package belongs to 'the correct borrower (Destiny Pham)'. It never mentions Sandra Kang or any borrower mismatch between the LOS record and the package.
9 The final response states that the last reliable disclosure on LN-2026-00613 is the original 30-year Loan Estimate, not the current CD package.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✓✓ 50%
✗Run #1The final response discusses LN-2026-00623 (Vincent Pham), not LN-2026-00613. There is no mention of the original 30-year Loan Estimate as the last reliable disclosure. The agent worked on the wrong loan.
✗Run #2The final response states 'There isn't one' as the last reliable disclosure, meaning the agent concluded there is no reliable disclosure at all rather than identifying the original 30-year LE as the last reliable one. The criteria requires stating the 30-year LE is the last reliable disclosure.
✗Run #3The final response discusses LN-2026-00623, not LN-2026-00613. It never identifies a '30-year Loan Estimate' as the last reliable disclosure. It discusses a different loan entirely.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states: 'The original 30yr Loan Estimate is the only valid disclosure on this file' as the last reliable disclosure.
✓Run #5The final response states 'Last reliable disclosure: The original 30-year Loan Estimate. That is the only valid disclosure on file.' The current CD is explicitly flagged as invalid.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states 'Last reliable disclosure: The original Loan Estimate on 30-year fixed terms. That's the only valid disclosure in the file.'
10 The final response states that the current CD package should not be relied on until the borrower/file mismatch is resolved.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The response does state that the current CD package should not be relied on due to borrower identification and rate lock issues. While operating on the wrong loan, this substantive statement matches the criterion.
✓Run #2The final response clearly states the current CD package 'was built against the stale 30yr LE' and that 'the entire disclosure stack is broken' with a borrower/loan number mismatch, making clear the CD package should not be relied on until the file is reconciled.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states that the current CD package cannot be relied on due to the borrower name mismatch (Destiny Pham vs Vincent Pham) and expired rate lock, until the file is corrected.
✓Run #4The final response states the package identifies the wrong borrower and 'must be reconciled before any corrected disclosure can go out,' and the email to Catherine Grayson says not to use the current package.
✓Run #5The final response states 'Until the borrower data is reconciled in the LOS, no disclosure work can proceed,' directly addressing the mismatch issue with the package at title.
✗Run #6The final response does not identify a borrower/file mismatch. It states the package is for the correct borrower and correct loan but with invalid disclosure figures. There is no mention of the file needing reconciliation due to a borrower identity mismatch.
11 The final response states that borrower or title communications tied to the current package should be treated as superseded until the file is reconciled.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The response explicitly states 'Any borrower or title communication that references the original rate of 6.641% or the March 23 closing date is superseded,' and email/wire communications with stale figures are identified as superseded.
✓Run #2The final response states Carlos's borrower delay notice is 'directionally correct but cited the wrong loan number,' and that the CD package is superseded. The email reply explicitly tells Catherine Grayson to treat the package as superseded and not use it for borrower communications.
✓Run #3The final response states: 'Sofia's hold email is unreliable as an external control... Any CD prep or distribution that continued after that email cannot be assumed to have been properly held,' treating prior communications as superseded.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states: 'Sofia's email to Catherine Grayson and Carlos's delay email to 'Destiny' both carry incorrect borrower information and must be treated as superseded.'
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'Sofia's hold email to Catherine Grayson, Carlos's delay email to the borrower, and the CD package at Grayson Law all have to be treated as superseded. Nothing in the current closing package is usable.'
✓Run #6The final response states 'The CD sent to Catherine Grayson's office, and any closing figures distributed to title/agents/borrower based on that CD, are all superseded.'
12 The final response states that the rate lock for LN-2026-00613 expired on 2026-04-14.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✓✗ 33%

13 The final response states that the expired rate lock creates a separate timing problem that affects the correction sequence before the file can proceed.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The response explicitly states 'The lock must be re-established with Plaza Home Mortgage before a corrected CD can be generated,' correctly identifying the lock as a sequencing blocker that changes what must happen first.
✓Run #2The final response clearly states 'No corrected disclosures can be built until we have a valid rate from a lock extension or relock,' establishing that the expired rate lock must be resolved first before the correction sequence can proceed.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states that before a corrected CD can go out, Plaza Home Mortgage must provide new lock/extension pricing — identifying the expired lock as a separate sequencing problem affecting the correction workflow.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'The corrected disclosure sequence (revised LE → corrected CD → 3 business day wait) has to complete before closing, and the lock must be extended or relocked before that matters,' indicating the expired lock creates a separate timing dependency.
✓Run #5The final response has a dedicated 'Rate lock changes the sequence' section stating 'We cannot issue a corrected LE with accurate pricing until the lock is extended or reestablished with UWM. Lock comes before disclosures.'
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states 'Lock timing drives the sequence: The lock expired 3/23. You cannot issue corrected disclosures until pricing is confirmed, so relock or extension must happen first.'

✓Run #6The final response states the correction sequence as 'revised LE → corrected CD → new 3 business day wait → close', clearly indicating a revised LE must precede the corrected CD.

outcome
